---
layout: default
title: "Political Standoff Over NEET and Legislative Reforms"
date: 2026-07-24T15:51:32.708820+00:00
summary: "Parliament faces repeated adjournments as the opposition demands a PM address on NEET paper leaks while the Cabinet approves new anti-paper leak legislation"
run_time_ist: "9:21PM"
---

<article class="digest-post">
  <a class="back-link" href="{{ '/' | relative_url }}">Political Brief</a>
  <p class="post-meta">Gemini Summary: 9:21PM</p>


<h2 class="section-title">India National Politics</h2>

<ul class="digest-points">
  <li><strong>Parliamentary deadlock:</strong> Both Houses of Parliament were adjourned until Monday as the opposition continued to protest over the NEET paper leak row and the government&#x27;s refusal to hold a debate under opposition-set conditions.<span class="source-chips"><a href="https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMivgFBVV95cUxON0h3QVI3MEhxdkJiU2Z6SG14ZV9jZXFDSkVGUDJRYnFlcFVYT2UzeFJ2S3FmS3I4dlJleWRraHdES2NXOTJQcTlBYWhRYnZjR3h4X2JqWGZsVGNvQ0YyNUdUdDhkMmlHdUtGQVZpM2l1blV0YWdOeGdzOXU5d2FsQ1VyRXE4dmZKTjhjb29tVEVja005YUNlMFpyQ3FrdldIWWZIUmJPWmpXWk1NOFotWlA1bmRHM3ZzWGxUdzd30gG-AUFVX3lxTE43SHdBUjcwSHF2QmJTZnpIbXhlX2NlcUNKRUZQMlFicWVwVVhPZTN4UnZLcWZLcjh2UmV5ZGtod0RLY1c5MlBxOUFhaFFidmNHeHhfYmpYZmxUY29DRjI1R1R0OGQyaUd1S0ZBVmkzaXVuVXRhZ054Z3M5dTl3YWxDVXJFcTh2ZkpOOGNvb21URWNrTTlhQ2UwWnJDcWt2V0hZZkhSYk9aaldaTU04Wi1aUDVuZEczdnNYbFR3N3c?oc=5&amp;hl=en-US&amp;gl=US&amp;ceid=US%3Aen" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">I18</a> <a href="https://news.google.?oc=5&amp;hl=en-US&amp;gl=US&amp;ceid=US%3Aen" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">I34</a> <a href="https://news.google.?oc=5&amp;hl=en-US&amp;gl=US&amp;ceid=US%3Aen" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">I35</a></span></li>
  <li><strong>Supreme Court intervention:</strong> The Supreme Court has demanded accountability from the Centre regarding NEET paper leaks, warning against a &quot;series of errors&quot; and scheduling a hearing for Monday to address allegations of police excesses against student protesters.<span class="source-chips"><a href="https://news.google.?oc=5&amp;hl=en-US&amp;gl=US&amp;ceid=US%3Aen" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">I20</a> <a href="https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMilAFBVV95cUxNMXZrb2JwWVYzcXpYNFdRZWNqMG5Mbkhtbl84TVpKSl84T05IZmlUN3h0UHd2Nkk4U3pIZ3pBMnFoWVpYTm9jQ2FMSVh0d3lvbXc5MDZzNE9XaElPNFNmRUVvZ1VYRnpKaHBJVjVtaHRlWXFJdnQ4Nk9GQnBqbnh4TTgxQVZ3THBTQjEySzVSZlMybDRY?oc=5&amp;hl=en-US&amp;gl=US&amp;ceid=US%3Aen" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">I21</a> <a href="https://news.google.?oc=5&amp;hl=en-US&amp;gl=US&amp;ceid=US%3Aen" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">I22</a> <a href="https://news.google.?oc=5&amp;hl=en-US&amp;gl=US&amp;ceid=US%3Aen" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">I23</a> <a href="https://news.google.?oc=5&amp;hl=en-US&amp;gl=US&amp;ceid=US%3Aen" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">I24</a> <a href="https://news.google.?oc=5&amp;hl=en-US&amp;gl=US&amp;ceid=US%3Aen" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">I25</a> <a href="https://news.google.?oc=5&amp;hl=en-US&amp;gl=US&amp;ceid=US%3Aen" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">I32</a> <a href="https://news.google.?oc=5&amp;hl=en-US&amp;gl=US&amp;ceid=US%3Aen" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">I33</a></span></li>
  <li><strong>Opposition demands:</strong> Congress leaders, including Mallikarjun Kharge, have urged the Prime Minister to address the NEET crisis directly in Parliament rather than through &quot;one-sided&quot; broadcasts, while negotiations continue regarding the demand for a minister&#x27;s resignation.<span class="source-chips"><a href="https://news.google.?oc=5&amp;hl=en-US&amp;gl=US&amp;ceid=US%3Aen" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">I8</a> <a href="https://news.google.?oc=5&amp;hl=en-US&amp;gl=US&amp;ceid=US%3Aen" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">I9</a> <a href="https://news.google.?oc=5&amp;hl=en-US&amp;gl=US&amp;ceid=US%3Aen" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">I10</a> <a href="https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMiwwFBVV95cUxQLUNGMEdVYm9kVmhRNXhzOWhwUEl0b3QzczJvd1JXQjhYa1ZGOHp6ZUVWSE4wSVctdjRieE5la0c2M2hZWFFiX3NrNHF3ZFhVRUVDOEd1a2JMUGVIQkhFUklybGVKTVFoN05YZHJ5cl9HN09ZN3NtZ0xpQlJrWlRaWVNsQ1ZHNkdfbENQUllrWV9LcUJaclVxTGhsVDR0UW1ZUm5uZ1Z5OWdkQnNva0pGLV8wd3hjTzBEaGwtZGh3YlBwS0E?oc=5&amp;hl=en-US&amp;gl=US&amp;ceid=US%3Aen" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">I26</a> <a href="https://news.google.?oc=5&amp;hl=en-US&amp;gl=US&amp;ceid=US%3Aen" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">I27</a> <a href="https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMifkFVX3lxTE8xUGw3RmJ5WHBRem9zVk1DMmJ2RTdMVzlYTTJVdDU0NldnaktKYU03WFd4SUwzSENvT2REelFKNW9ZTTBUSVROSWhxbkxGWllyb1psSXNOSjZZZjdBc1BRdkxyZ20ycmRLcTdEaC1HSlMyTjgwbzNyRDZhMElxUQ?oc=5&amp;hl=en-US&amp;gl=US&amp;ceid=US%3Aen" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">I28</a> <a href="https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMijAFBVV95cUxQQ2Faa2tTd0VOQXNIY1BxYXM0QnhxMUlOREVoeENCV2xybHZ1VC1kR0xqcVlSbk5jdXdmVEtDMlhzZGhqRncwc1RQYi1zVHRsazVXOGR3NjdpSF9hbHZQcHV3RElWbzlqRi14OVpKWXBjaUg4UTNWRU1vbVkzT0tKNnNCd056OHlaQ0RTVQ?oc=5&amp;hl=en-US&amp;gl=US&amp;ceid=US%3Aen" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">I29</a> <a href="https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMixgFBVV95cUxPTXVrMFdyVEZUYXBXMEgxSmRkU3pDV0hhYm5FeUVPUlRDVUo4WnVnOW85cmhtQWNKX0duNFNfNFdwMlBfZzkwV2I5ODI0bG15QUJKMHVrVW10Y2xBYWlNUVIxenRkd0gtNVZvczRERG1LczJ3VzFReXpYd2tnTmg0X1Fwamw3N2NJdkJqLThBSXpDbDBZelVvYjlHU0Rnd2ozd1ExdHZqWDlKQzg5dW5WTmx5eVI0aWM2cGtUVWZwV1p1MXA2S1E?oc=5&amp;hl=en-US&amp;gl=US&amp;ceid=US%3Aen" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">I30</a> <a href="https://news.google.?oc=5&amp;hl=en-US&amp;gl=US&amp;ceid=US%3Aen" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">I31</a></span></li>
</ul>

<h2 class="section-title">Policy and Institutions</h2>

<ul class="digest-points">
  <li><strong>Anti-paper leak legislation:</strong> The Union Cabinet has approved amendments to the anti-paper leak law, introducing harsher penalties for offenders, with the government expected to table the bill in Parliament next week.<span class="source-chips"><a href="https://news.google.?oc=5&amp;hl=en-US&amp;gl=US&amp;ceid=US%3Aen" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">W14</a> <a href="https://news.google.?oc=5&amp;hl=en-US&amp;gl=US&amp;ceid=US%3Aen" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">W9</a></span></li>
  <li><strong>Judicial and legislative updates:</strong> The Supreme Court has directed the Centre to amend the National Highways Act for judicial land acquisition oversight, while the Lok Sabha prepares to discuss the Supreme Court Judges Amendment Bill.<span class="source-chips"><a href="https://news.google.?oc=5&amp;hl=en-US&amp;gl=US&amp;ceid=US%3Aen" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">W9</a> <a href="https://news.google.?oc=5&amp;hl=en-US&amp;gl=US&amp;ceid=US%3Aen" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">W8</a></span></li>
</ul>
